{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_2d3f57c0a8c84ed6834b19a0","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::Result;\n4\tuse serde_json::Value;\n5\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n6\t\n7\t/// Result merger: combines responses from multiple shards.\n8\tpub trait Merger: Send + Sync {\n9\t    /// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n10\t    ///\n11\t    /// Takes the raw JSON responses from each shard and produces\n12\t    /// a merged result with global sorting, offset/limit applied,\n13\t    /// and facet aggregation.\n14\t    fn merge(\n15\t        &self,\n16\t        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n17\t        offset: usize,\n18\t        limit: usize,\n19\t        client_requested_score: bool,\n20\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult>;\n21\t}\n22\t\n23\t/// Response from a single shard.\n24\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n25\tpub struct ShardResponse {\n26\t    /// Shard identifier.\n27\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n28\t\n29\t    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n30\t    pub body: Value,\n31\t\n32\t    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n33\t    pub success: bool,\n34\t}\n35\t\n36\t/// Merged search result.\n37\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n38\tpub struct MergedResult {\n39\t    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n40\t    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n41\t\n42\t    /// Aggregated facets.\n43\t    pub facets: Value,\n44\t\n45\t    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n46\t    pub total_hits: u64,\n47\t\n48\t    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n49\t    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n50\t\n51\t    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n52\t    pub degraded: bool,\n53\t}\n54\t\n55\t/// Default implementation of Merger.\n56\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n57\tpub struct MergerImpl;\n58\t\n59\timpl Merger for MergerImpl {\n60\t    fn merge(\n61\t        &self,\n62\t        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n63\t        offset: usize,\n64\t        limit: usize,\n65\t        client_requested_score: bool,\n66\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n67\t        // Filter to only successful responses\n68\t        let successful_shards: Vec<_> = shard_responses.iter().filter(|s| s.success).collect();\n69\t\n70\t        // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n71\t        let degraded = successful_shards.len() < shard_responses.len();\n72\t\n73\t        // Collect all hits with their ranking scores\n74\t        let mut all_hits: Vec<(f64, Value)> = Vec::new();\n75\t\n76\t        for shard in &successful_shards {\n77\t            if let Some(hits) = shard.body.get(\"hits\").and_then(|h| h.as_array()) {\n78\t                for hit in hits {\n79\t                    // Extract ranking score\n80\t                    let score = hit\n81\t                        .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n82\t                        .and_then(|s| s.as_f64())\n83\t                        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n84\t\n85\t                    all_hits.push((score, hit.clone()));\n86\t                }\n87\t            }\n88\t        }\n89\t\n90\t        // Sort globally by score descending\n91\t        all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| b.0.partial_cmp(&a.0).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n92\t\n93\t        // Apply offset and limit\n94\t        let page: Vec<Value> = all_hits\n95\t            .into_iter()\n96\t            .skip(offset)\n97\t            .take(limit)\n98\t            .map(|(_, mut hit)| {\n99\t                // Strip _miroir_shard (always removed)\n100\t                if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n101\t                    obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n102\t                }\n103\t\n104\t                // Strip _rankingScore if client didn't request it\n105\t                if !client_requested_score {\n106\t                    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n107\t                        obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n108\t                    }\n109\t                }\n110\t\n111\t                hit\n112\t            })\n113\t            .collect();\n114\t\n115\t        // Aggregate facets across all shards\n116\t        let facets = merge_facets(&successful_shards);\n117\t\n118\t        // Sum estimated total hits\n119\t        let total_hits: u64 = successful_shards\n120\t            .iter()\n121\t            .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n122\t            .sum();\n123\t\n124\t        // Max processing time across all shards\n125\t        let processing_time_ms: u64 = successful_shards\n126\t            .iter()\n127\t            .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"processingTimeMs\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n128\t            .max()\n129\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n130\t\n131\t        Ok(MergedResult {\n132\t            hits: page,\n133\t            facets,\n134\t            total_hits,\n135\t            processing_time_ms,\n136\t            degraded,\n137\t        })\n138\t    }\n139\t}\n140\t\n141\t/// Merge facet distributions from all shards.\n142\t///\n143\t/// Facets are nested objects like `{\"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5}}`.\n144\t/// We sum counts for each facet value across all shards.\n145\tfn merge_facets(shards: &[&ShardResponse]) -> Value {\n146\t    let mut merged_facets: HashMap<String, HashMap<String, u64>> = HashMap::new();\n147\t\n148\t    for shard in shards {\n149\t        if let Some(facets) = shard\n150\t            .body\n151\t            .get(\"facetDistribution\")\n152\t            .and_then(|f| f.as_object())\n153\t        {\n154\t            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n155\t                if let Some(values_obj) = facet_values.as_object() {\n156\t                    let entry = merged_facets.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n157\t\n158\t                    for (value, count) in values_obj {\n159\t                        let count_val = count.as_u64().unwrap_or(0);\n160\t                        *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count_val;\n161\t                    }\n162\t                }\n163\t            }\n164\t        }\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    // Convert back to JSON structure\n168\t    let mut result = serde_json::Map::new();\n169\t    for (facet_name, values) in merged_facets {\n170\t        let values_obj: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = values\n171\t            .into_iter()\n172\t            .map(|(k, v)| (k, Value::Number(v.into())))\n173\t            .collect();\n174\t        result.insert(facet_name, Value::Object(values_obj));\n175\t    }\n176\t\n177\t    Value::Object(result)\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\t/// Stub implementation that returns empty results.\n181\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n182\tpub struct StubMerger;\n183\t\n184\timpl Merger for StubMerger {\n185\t    fn merge(\n186\t        &self,\n187\t        _shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n188\t        _offset: usize,\n189\t        _limit: usize,\n190\t        _client_requested_score: bool,\n191\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n192\t        Ok(MergedResult {\n193\t            hits: Vec::new(),\n194\t            facets: serde_json::json!({}),\n195\t            total_hits: 0,\n196\t            processing_time_ms: 0,\n197\t            degraded: false,\n198\t        })\n199\t    }\n200\t}\n201\t\n202\t#[cfg(test)]\n203\tmod tests {\n204\t    use super::*;\n205\t\n206\t    fn create_hit(score: f64, id: &str) -> Value {\n207\t        serde_json::json!({\n208\t            \"id\": id,\n209\t            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n210\t            \"_miroir_shard\": 0,\n211\t        })\n212\t    }\n213\t\n214\t    fn create_shard_response(shard_id: u32, hits: Vec<Value>, total: u64) -> ShardResponse {\n215\t        ShardResponse {\n216\t            shard_id,\n217\t            body: serde_json::json!({\n218\t                \"hits\": hits,\n219\t                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n220\t                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n221\t                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n222\t            }),\n223\t            success: true,\n224\t        }\n225\t    }\n226\t\n227\t    #[test]\n228\t    fn test_global_sort_by_ranking_score() {\n229\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n230\t\n231\t        let hits1 = vec![\n232\t            create_hit(0.5, \"doc1\"),\n233\t            create_hit(0.9, \"doc2\"),\n234\t            create_hit(0.3, \"doc3\"),\n235\t        ];\n236\t        let hits2 = vec![\n237\t            create_hit(0.7, \"doc4\"),\n238\t            create_hit(0.1, \"doc5\"),\n239\t            create_hit(0.8, \"doc6\"),\n240\t        ];\n241\t\n242\t        let shards = vec![\n243\t            create_shard_response(0, hits1, 3),\n244\t            create_shard_response(1, hits2, 3),\n245\t        ];\n246\t\n247\t        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n248\t\n249\t        // Check global ordering: should be doc2(0.9), doc6(0.8), doc4(0.7), doc1(0.5), doc3(0.3), doc5(0.1)\n250\t        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 6);\n251\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc2\");\n252\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc6\");\n253\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[2][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n254\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[3][\"id\"], \"doc1\");\n255\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[4][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n256\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[5][\"id\"], \"doc5\");\n257\t    }\n258\t\n259\t    #[test]\n260\t    fn test_offset_and_limit_applied_after_merge() {\n261\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n262\t\n263\t        let hits = vec![\n264\t            create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"),\n265\t            create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\"),\n266\t            create_hit(0.7, \"doc3\"),\n267\t            create_hit(0.6, \"doc4\"),\n268\t            create_hit(0.5, \"doc5\"),\n269\t        ];\n270\t\n271\t        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 5)];\n272\t\n273\t        let result = merger.merge(shards, 2, 2, false).unwrap();\n274\t\n275\t        // Should skip first 2, take next 2\n276\t        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n277\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n278\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n279\t    }\n280\t\n281\t    #[test]\n282\t    fn assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n606\t    }\n607\t}\n608\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"85a7c867-a77c-4970-863a-bd87698381eb","uuid":"2632dd3a-2363-45f0-a260-1b985fe8638f","timestamp":"2026-05-09T14:54:04.604Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/merger.rs","content":"//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n\nuse crate::Result;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Result merger: combines responses from multiple shards.\npub trait Merger: Send + Sync {\n    /// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n    ///\n    /// Takes the raw JSON responses from each shard and produces\n    /// a merged result with global sorting, offset/limit applied,\n    /// and facet aggregation.\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        offset: usize,\n        limit: usize,\n        client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult>;\n}\n\n/// Response from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ShardResponse {\n    /// Shard identifier.\n    pub shard_id: u32,\n\n    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n    pub body: Value,\n\n    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n    pub success: bool,\n}\n\n/// Merged search result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MergedResult {\n    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n\n    /// Aggregated facets.\n    pub facets: Value,\n\n    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n    pub total_hits: u64,\n\n    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n    pub degraded: bool,\n}\n\n/// Default implementation of Merger.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct MergerImpl;\n\nimpl Merger for MergerImpl {\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        offset: usize,\n        limit: usize,\n        client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n        // Filter to only successful responses\n        let successful_shards: Vec<_> = shard_responses.iter().filter(|s| s.success).collect();\n\n        // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n        let degraded = successful_shards.len() < shard_responses.len();\n\n        // Collect all hits with their ranking scores\n        let mut all_hits: Vec<(f64, Value)> = Vec::new();\n\n        for shard in &successful_shards {\n            if let Some(hits) = shard.body.get(\"hits\").and_then(|h| h.as_array()) {\n                for hit in hits {\n                    // Extract ranking score\n                    let score = hit\n                        .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n                        .and_then(|s| s.as_f64())\n                        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n\n                    all_hits.push((score, hit.clone()));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Sort globally by score descending\n        all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| b.0.partial_cmp(&a.0).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n\n        // Apply offset and limit\n        let page: Vec<Value> = all_hits\n            .into_iter()\n            .skip(offset)\n            .take(limit)\n            .map(|(_, mut hit)| {\n                // Strip _miroir_shard (always removed)\n                if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n                    obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n                }\n\n                // Strip _rankingScore if client didn't request it\n                if !client_requested_score {\n                    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n                        obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n                    }\n                }\n\n                hit\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        // Aggregate facets across all shards\n        let facets = merge_facets(&successful_shards);\n\n        // Sum estimated total hits\n        let total_hits: u64 = successful_shards\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n            .sum();\n\n        // Max processing time across all shards\n        let processing_time_ms: u64 = successful_shards\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"processingTimeMs\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n            .max()\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        Ok(MergedResult {\n            hits: page,\n            facets,\n            total_hits,\n            processing_time_ms,\n            degraded,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Merge facet distributions from all shards.\n///\n/// Facets are nested objects like `{\"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5}}`.\n/// We sum counts for each facet value across all shards.\nfn merge_facets(shards: &[&ShardResponse]) -> Value {\n    let mut merged_facets: HashMap<String, HashMap<String, u64>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for shard in shards {\n        if let Some(facets) = shard\n            .body\n            .get(\"facetDistribution\")\n            .and_then(|f| f.as_object())\n        {\n            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n                if let Some(values_obj) = facet_values.as_object() {\n                    let entry = merged_facets.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n\n                    for (value, count) in values_obj {\n                        let count_val = count.as_u64().unwrap_or(0);\n                        *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count_val;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Convert back to JSON structure\n    let mut result = serde_json::Map::new();\n    for (facet_name, values) in merged_facets {\n        let values_obj: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = values\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|(k, v)| (k, Value::Number(v.into())))\n            .collect();\n        result.insert(facet_name, Value::Object(values_obj));\n    }\n\n    Value::Object(result)\n}\n\n/// Stub implementation that returns empty results.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct StubMerger;\n\nimpl Merger for StubMerger {\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        _shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        _offset: usize,\n        _limit: usize,\n        _client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n        Ok(MergedResult {\n            hits: Vec::new(),\n            facets: serde_json::json!({}),\n            total_hits: 0,\n            processing_time_ms: 0,\n            degraded: false,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    fn create_hit(score: f64, id: &str) -> Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"id\": id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n            \"_miroir_shard\": 0,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn create_shard_response(shard_id: u32, hits: Vec<Value>, total: u64) -> ShardResponse {\n        ShardResponse {\n            shard_id,\n            body: serde_json::json!({\n                \"hits\": hits,\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n            }),\n            success: true,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_sort_by_ranking_score() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits1 = vec![\n            create_hit(0.5, \"doc1\"),\n            create_hit(0.9, \"doc2\"),\n            create_hit(0.3, \"doc3\"),\n        ];\n        let hits2 = vec![\n            create_hit(0.7, \"doc4\"),\n            create_hit(0.1, \"doc5\"),\n            create_hit(0.8, \"doc6\"),\n        ];\n\n        let shards = vec![\n            create_shard_response(0, hits1, 3),\n            create_shard_response(1, hits2, 3),\n        ];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        // Check global ordering: should be doc2(0.9), doc6(0.8), doc4(0.7), doc1(0.5), doc3(0.3), doc5(0.1)\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 6);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc6\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[2][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[3][\"id\"], \"doc1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[4][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[5][\"id\"], \"doc5\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_offset_and_limit_applied_after_merge() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![\n            create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"),\n            create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\"),\n            create_hit(0.7, \"doc3\"),\n            create_hit(0.6, \"doc4\"),\n            create_hit(0.5, \"doc5\"),\n        ];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 5)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 2, 2, false).unwrap();\n\n        // Should skip first 2, take next 2\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_ranking_score_stripped_when_not_requested() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"), create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\")];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 2)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        // _rankingScore should be stripped\n        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n        assert!(result.hits[1].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_ranking_score_included_when_requested() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"), create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\")];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 2)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, true).unwrap();\n\n        // _rankingScore should be present\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"_rankingScore\"], 0.9);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"_rankingScore\"], 0.8);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_miroir_shard_always_stripped() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\")];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 1)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, true).unwrap();\n\n        // _miroir_shard should always be stripped\n        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_facet_counts_summed_across_shards() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let shard1 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"color\": {\n                    \"red\": 10,\n                    \"blue\": 5,\n                },\n                \"size\": {\n                    \"large\": 8,\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        let shard2 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"color\": {\n                    \"red\": 7,\n                    \"green\": 3,\n                },\n                \"size\": {\n                    \"large\": 4,\n                    \"small\": 6,\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        let shards = vec![\n            ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: 0,\n                body: shard1,\n                success: true,\n            },\n            ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: 1,\n                body: shard2,\n                success: true,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        let result = {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_6c8980c597184f2da5e15828","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Topology management: node registry, groups, and health state.\n2\t\n3\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n4\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n5\tuse std::fmt;\n6\t\n7\t/// Unique identifier for a node.\n8\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n9\tpub struct NodeId(String);\n10\t\n11\timpl NodeId {\n12\t    /// Create a new NodeId.\n13\t    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n14\t        Self(id)\n15\t    }\n16\t\n17\t    /// Get the node ID as a string slice.\n18\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n19\t        &self.0\n20\t    }\n21\t}\n22\t\n23\timpl From<String> for NodeId {\n24\t    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n25\t        Self(s)\n26\t    }\n27\t}\n28\t\n29\timpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n30\t    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n31\t        &self.0\n32\t    }\n33\t}\n34\t\n35\t/// Health status of a node.\n36\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n37\tpub enum NodeStatus {\n38\t    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n39\t    Healthy,\n40\t    /// Node is degraded (intermittent failures, still serving traffic).\n41\t    Degraded,\n42\t    /// Node is active and fully operational (synonym for Healthy).\n43\t    Active,\n44\t    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n45\t    Joining,\n46\t    /// Node is draining (graceful shutdown, not accepting new writes).\n47\t    Draining,\n48\t    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n49\t    Failed,\n50\t    /// Node has been removed from the cluster (tracked for migration).\n51\t    Removed,\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\timpl NodeStatus {\n55\t    /// Check if a transition from `self` to `new_status` is valid.\n56\t    ///\n57\t    /// # State Transition Rules\n58\t    ///\n59\t    /// | From | To | Triggered by |\n60\t    /// |------|-----|-------------|\n61\t    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |\n62\t    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |\n63\t    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |\n64\t    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |\n65\t    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |\n66\t    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |\n67\t    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |\n68\t    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |\n69\t    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {\n70\t        match (self, new_status) {\n71\t            // Initial state\n72\t            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n73\t\n74\t            // Normal operations\n75\t            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,\n76\t            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,\n77\t\n78\t            // Failure and recovery\n79\t            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n80\t            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n81\t            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n82\t\n83\t            // Degradation\n84\t            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n85\t            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n86\t            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n87\t\n88\t            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)\n89\t            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n90\t            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,\n91\t\n92\t            // Same state is always valid\n93\t            (s, t) if s == t => true,\n94\t\n95\t            // All other transitions are invalid\n96\t            _ => false,\n97\t        }\n98\t    }\n99\t\n100\t    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.\n101\t    ///\n102\t    /// # Write Eligibility Rules\n103\t    ///\n104\t    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:\n105\t    ///\n106\t    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |\n107\t    /// |--------|----------------|-------|\n108\t    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |\n109\t    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |\n110\t    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |\n111\t    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |\n112\t    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |\n113\t    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |\n114\t    ///\n115\t    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node\n116\t    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard IS being actively migrated off this node\n117\t    /// (use `None` if the shard is not being drained or no shard is being checked).\n118\t    /// When `Some(...)`, the node is NOT eligible for writes.\n119\t    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n120\t        match self {\n121\t            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n122\t            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n123\t            NodeStatus::Draining => !draining_shard.is_some(),\n124\t        }\n125\t    }\n126\t}\n127\t\n128\timpl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {\n129\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n130\t        match self {\n131\t            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \"healthy\"),\n132\t            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \"degraded\"),\n133\t            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n134\t            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \"joining\"),\n135\t            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \"draining\"),\n136\t            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),\n137\t            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \"removed\"),\n138\t        }\n139\t    }\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n143\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n144\tpub struct Node {\n145\t    /// Unique node identifier.\n146\t    pub id: NodeId,\n147\t\n148\t    /// Node base URL / address.\n149\t    pub address: String,\n150\t\n151\t    /// Current health status.\n152\t    pub status: NodeStatus,\n153\t\n154\t    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n155\t    pub replica_group: u32,\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\timpl Node {\n159\t    /// Create a new node.\n160\t    pub fn new(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n161\t        Self {\n162\t            id,\n163\t            address,\n164\t            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n165\t            replica_group,\n166\t        }\n167\t    }\n168\t\n169\t    /// Create a new node with a specific status.\n170\t    pub fn with_status(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32, status: NodeStatus) -> Self {\n171\t        Self {\n172\t            id,\n173\t            address,\n174\t            status,\n175\t            replica_group,\n176\t        }\n177\t    }\n178\t\n179\t    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n180\t    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n181\t        matches!(self.status, NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active)\n182\t    }\n183\t\n184\t    /// Transition the node to a new status, validating the transition.\n185\t    ///\n186\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if the transition is valid, `Err` otherwise.\n187\t    pub fn set_status(&mut self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> Result<(), TransitionError> {\n188\t        if self.status.can_transition_to(new_status) {\n189\t            self.status = new_status;\n190\t            Ok(())\n191\t        } else {\n192\t            Err(TransitionError {\n193\t                from: self.status,\n194\t                to: new_status,\n195\t            })\n196\t        }\n197\t    }\n198\t\n199\t    /// Check if the node is eligible to receive writes for a specific shard.\n200\t    ///\n201\t    /// For nodes in `Draining` status, this depends on whether the shard is\n202\t    /// being actively migrated off this node. The caller should pass\n203\t    /// `Some(shard_id)` if the shard is being drained from this node.\n204\t    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(&self, shard_id: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n205\t        self.status.is_write_eligible_for(shard_id)\n206\t    }\n207\t}\n208\t\n209\t/// Error returned when an invalid state transition is attempted.\n210\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n211\tpub struct TransitionError {\n212\t    pub from: NodeStatus,\n213\t    pub to: NodeStatus,\n214\t}\n215\t\n216\timpl fmt::Display for TransitionError {\n217\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n218\t        write!(\n219\t            f,\n220\t            \"invalid state transition from {} to {}\",\n221\t            self.from, self.to\n222\t        )\n223\t    }\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\timpl std::error::Error for TransitionError {}\n227\t\n228\t/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n229\t///\n230\t/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n231\t/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n232\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n233\tpub struct Group {\n234\t    /// Group identifier (0-based).\n235\t    pub id: u32,\n236\t\n237\t    /// Nodes in this group.\n238\t    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n239\t}\n240\t\n241\timpl Group {\n242\t    /// Create a new group.\n243\t    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n244\t        Self {\n245\t            id,\n246\t            nodes: Vec::new(),\n247\t        }\n248\t    }\n249\t\n250\t    /// Add a node to this group.\n251\t    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n252\t        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n253\t            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n254\t        }\n255\t    }\n256\t\n257\t    /// Get the node IDs in this group.\n258\t    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n259\t        &self.nodes\n260\t    }\n261\t\n262\t    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n263\t    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n264\t        self.nodes.len()\n265\t    }\n266\t\n267\t    /// Get all healthy nodes in this group, looking them up from the topology.\n268\t    ///\n269\t    /// This requires access to the topology's node map to resolve NodeIds to Nodes.\n270\t    pub fn healthy_nodes<'a>(&'a self, all_nodes: &'a HashMap<NodeId, Node>) -> Vec<&'a Node> {\n271\t        self.nodes\n272\t            .iter()\n273\t            .filter_map(|node_id| all_nodes.get(node_id))\n274\t            .filter(|node| node.is_healthy())\n275\t            .collect()\n276\t    }\n277\t}\n278\t\n279\t/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n280\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n281\tpub struct Topology {\n282\t    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n283\t    nodes: HashMap<NodeId, Node>,\n284\t\n285\t    /// Replica groups.\n286\t    groups: Vec<Group>,\n287\t\n288\t    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n289\t    rf: usize,\n290\t\n291\t    /// Total number of logical shards (S).\n292\t    shards: u32,\n293\t}\n294\t\n295\timpl Topology {\n296\t    /// Create a new empty topology.\n297\t    pub fn new(shards: u32, rf: usize) -> Self {\n298\t        Self {\n299\t            nodes: HashMap::new(),\n300\t            groups: Vec::new(),\n301\t            rf,\n302\t            shards,\n303\t        }\n304\t    }\n305\t\n306\t    /// Add a node to the topology.\n307\t    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n308\t        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n309\t\n310\t        // Ensure group exists\n311\t        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n312\t            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n313\t        }\n314\t\n315\t        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n316\t        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), node);\n317\t    }\n318\t\n319\t    /// Get a node by ID.\n320\t    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n321\t        self.nodes.get(id)\n322\t    }\n323\t\n324\t    /// Get a mutable reference to a node by ID.\n325\t    pub fn node_mut(&mut self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&mut Node> {\n326\t        self.nodes.get_mut(id)\n327\t    }\n328\t\n329\t    /// Get all nodes.\n330\t    pub        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n337\t    }\n338\t\n339\t    /// Iterate over all groups.\n340\t    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n341\t        self.groups.iter()\n342\t as u32\n357\t    }\n358\t\n359\t    /// Get healthy nodes in a specific group.\n360\t    pub fn healthy_nodes_in_group(&self, group_id: u32) -> Vec<&Node> {\n361\t        self.group(group_id)\n362\t            .map(|g| g.healthy_nodes(&self.nodes))\n363\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n364\t    }\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t#[cfg(test)]\n368\tmod tests {\n369\t    use super::*;\n370\t\n371\t    // --- group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n415\t        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 1);\n416\t\n417\t        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()));\n418\t // Empty topology has 0 groups\n430\t        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 0);\n431\t\n432\t        // Add nodes to group 0\n433\t        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n434\t            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n435\t            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n436\t            0,\n437\t        ));\n438\t        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 1);\n439\t\n440\t        // Add nodes to group 1\n441\t        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n442\t            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n443\t            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n444\t            1,\n445\t        ));\n446\t        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n447\t\n448\t        // Add more nodes to existing groups\n449\t        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n450\t            NodeId::new(\"node3\".to_string()),\n451\t            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n452\t            0,\n453\t        ));\n454\t        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n455\t    }\n456\t\n457\t    #[test]\n458\t    fn test_topology_nodes_iter() {\n459\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 1);\n460\t\n461\t assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Failed.to_string(), \"failed\");\n806\t        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Removed.to_string(), \"removed\");\n807\t    }\n808\t\n809\t    #[test]\n810\t    fn assert!(msg.contains(\"draining\"));\n819\t    }\n820\t}\n821\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"85a7c867-a77c-4970-863a-bd87698381eb","uuid":"8c3cb6ee-d7b1-498c-93c2-8d197c4ad185","timestamp":"2026-05-09T14:54:04.613Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/topology.rs","content":"//! Topology management: node registry, groups, and health state.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Unique identifier for a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeId(String);\n\nimpl NodeId {\n    /// Create a new NodeId.\n    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n        Self(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node ID as a string slice.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<String> for NodeId {\n    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n        Self(s)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\n/// Health status of a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum NodeStatus {\n    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n    Healthy,\n    /// Node is degraded (intermittent failures, still serving traffic).\n    Degraded,\n    /// Node is active and fully operational (synonym for Healthy).\n    Active,\n    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n    Joining,\n    /// Node is draining (graceful shutdown, not accepting new writes).\n    Draining,\n    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n    Failed,\n    /// Node has been removed from the cluster (tracked for migration).\n    Removed,\n}\n\nimpl NodeStatus {\n    /// Check if a transition from `self` to `new_status` is valid.\n    ///\n    /// # State Transition Rules\n    ///\n    /// | From | To | Triggered by |\n    /// |------|-----|-------------|\n    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |\n    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |\n    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |\n    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |\n    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |\n    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |\n    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {\n        match (self, new_status) {\n            // Initial state\n            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Normal operations\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,\n\n            // Failure and recovery\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Degradation\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)\n            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,\n\n            // Same state is always valid\n            (s, t) if s == t => true,\n\n            // All other transitions are invalid\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.\n    ///\n    /// # Write Eligibility Rules\n    ///\n    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:\n    ///\n    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |\n    /// |--------|----------------|-------|\n    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |\n    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |\n    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |\n    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |\n    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |\n    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |\n    ///\n    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node\n    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard IS being actively migrated off this node\n    /// (use `None` if the shard is not being drained or no shard is being checked).\n    /// When `Some(...)`, the node is NOT eligible for writes.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n            NodeStatus::Draining => !draining_shard.is_some(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \"healthy\"),\n            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \"degraded\"),\n            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \"joining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \"draining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),\n            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \"removed\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Node {\n    /// Unique node identifier.\n    pub id: NodeId,\n\n    /// Node base URL / address.\n    pub address: String,\n\n    /// Current health status.\n    pub status: NodeStatus,\n\n    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n    pub replica_group: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Node {\n    /// Create a new node.\n    pub fn new(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new node with a specific status.\n    pub fn with_status(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32, status: NodeStatus) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.status, NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active)\n    }\n\n    /// Transition the node to a new status, validating the transition.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(())` if the transition is valid, `Err` otherwise.\n    pub fn set_status(&mut self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> Result<(), TransitionError> {\n        if self.status.can_transition_to(new_status) {\n            self.status = new_status;\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(TransitionError {\n                from: self.status,\n                to: new_status,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is eligible to receive writes for a specific shard.\n    ///\n    /// For nodes in `Draining` status, this depends on whether the shard is\n    /// being actively migrated off this node. The caller should pass\n    /// `Some(shard_id)` if the shard is being drained from this node.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(&self, shard_id: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        self.status.is_write_eligible_for(shard_id)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error returned when an invalid state transition is attempted.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct TransitionError {\n    pub from: NodeStatus,\n    pub to: NodeStatus,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for TransitionError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"invalid state transition from {} to {}\",\n            self.from, self.to\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for TransitionError {}\n\n/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n///\n/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Group {\n    /// Group identifier (0-based).\n    pub id: u32,\n\n    /// Nodes in this group.\n    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n}\n\nimpl Group {\n    /// Create a new group.\n    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            nodes: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to this group.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node IDs in this group.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n        &self.nodes\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.nodes.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Get all healthy nodes in this group, looking them up from the topology.\n    ///\n    /// This requires access to the topology's node map to resolve NodeIds to Nodes.\n    pub fn healthy_nodes<'a>(&'a self, all_nodes: &'a HashMap<NodeId, Node>) -> Vec<&'a Node> {\n        self.nodes\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|node_id| all_nodes.get(node_id))\n            .filter(|node| node.is_healthy())\n            .collect()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Topology {\n    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n    nodes: HashMap<NodeId, Node>,\n\n    /// Replica groups.\n    groups: Vec<Group>,\n\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n    rf: usize,\n\n    /// Total number of logical shards (S).\n    shards: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Topology {\n    /// Create a new empty topology.\n    pub fn new(shards: u32, rf: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            nodes: HashMap::new(),\n            groups: Vec::new(),\n            rf,\n            shards,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to the topology.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n\n        // Ensure group exists\n        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n        }\n\n        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), node);\n    }\n\n    /// Get a node by ID.\n    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n        self.nodes.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a mutable reference to a node by ID.\n    pub fn node_mut(&mut self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&mut Node> {\n        self.nodes.get_mut(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all nodes.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n        self.nodes.values()\n    }\n\n    /// Get a group by ID.\n    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over all groups.\n    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n        self.groups.iter()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the replication factor.\n    pub fn rf(&self) -> usize {\n        self.rf\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of shards.\n    pub fn shards(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.shards\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of replica groups.\n    pub fn replica_group_count(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.groups.len() as u32\n    }\n\n    /// Get healthy nodes in a specific group.\n    pub fn healthy_nodes_in_group(&self, group_id: u32) -> Vec<&Node> {\n        self.group(group_id)\n            .map(|g| g.healthy_nodes(&self.nodes))\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    // --- Existing tests updated for address field ---\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_is_healthy() {\n        let mut node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        );\n\n        // Joining status is not healthy\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Healthy status is healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Healthy;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Active status is healthy (synonym for Healthy)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Active;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Degraded status is not healthy (intermittent failures)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Degraded;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Draining status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Draining;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Failed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Failed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Removed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Removed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_group_node_count() {\n        let mut group = Group::new(0);\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 0);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 1);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n\n        // Adding duplicate node doesn't increase count\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_replica_group_count() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2);\n\n        // Empty topology has 0 groups\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 0);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 0\n assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 1);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 1\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n\n        // Add more nodes to existing groups\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_cdb105f62667496099fb7f83","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\n4\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n5\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n6\t\n7\t/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n8\t///\n9\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n10\t///\n11\t/// CRITICAL: Uses seed 0 to match Meilisearch Enterprise's hash function.\n12\t/// Any deviation (different seed, different ordering, endianness) forks\n13\t/// routing across any two Miroir instances and silently corrupts writes.\n14\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n15\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n16\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n17\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n18\t    h.finish()\n19\t}\n20\t\n21\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n22\t///\n23\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n24\t///\n25\t/// Nodes are sorted by score descending, with ties broken lexicographically\n26\t/// by node_id to ensure deterministic assignment even when hash scores collide.\n27\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n28\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n29\t        .iter()\n30\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n31\t        .collect();\n32\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n33\t        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n34\t            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n35\t    });\n36\t    scored\n37\t        .into_iter()\n38\t        .take(rf)\n39\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n40\t        .collect()\n41\t}\n42\t\n43\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n44\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n45\t    topology\n46\t        .groups()\n47\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n48\t        .collect()\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n52\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n53\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n57\t///\n58\t/// Returns a Vec where index i contains the node to query for shard i.\n59\t/// The length is always exactly shard_count, even if multiple shards\n60\t/// map to the same node.\n61\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n62\t    (0..shard_count)\n63\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n64\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n65\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n66\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n67\t        })\n68\t        .collect()\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n72\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n73\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n74\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n75\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\t#[cfg(test)]\n79\tmod tests {\n80\t    use super::*;\n81\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n82\t\n83\t    // Test 1: Rendezvous assignment is shard_count as usize);\n331\t\n332\t        // All nodes should be from the group\n333\t        for node in &covering {\n334\t            assert!(group.nodes().contains(node));\n335\t        }\n336\t    }\n337\t\n338\t    // Test 10: covering_set handles intra-group replica rotation\n339\t    #[test]\n340\t    fn test_covering_set_replica_rotation() {\n341\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n342\t        let group_id = 0;\n343\t\n344\t        // Add 3 nodes to a single group\n345\t        for node_idx in 0..3 {\n346\t            let node = );\n351\t            topology.add_node(node);\n352\t        }\n353\t\n354\t        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n355\t        let shard_count = 10;\n356\t        let rf = 2;\n357\t\n358\t        let covering_0 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 0);\n359\t        let covering_1 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 1);\n360\t\n361\t        // With RF=2, the covering set should rotate between the two replicas\n362\t        // For each shard, the node should be different between query_seq 0 and 1\n363\t        // Note: This is true for most shards but not all, since assignment is deterministic\n364\t        let mut rotated_count = 0;\n365\t        for (n0, n1) in covering_0.iter().zip(covering_1.iter()) {\n366\t            if n0 != n1 {\n367\t                rotated_count += 1;\n368\t            }\n369\t        }\n370\t\n371\t        // At least some shards should rotate (ideally most/all)\n372\t        assert!(\n373\t            rotated_count >= shard_count as usize / 2,\n374\t            \"Expected at least half of shards to rotate, but only {rotated_count} did\"\n375\t        );\n376\t    }\n377\t\n378\t    // Test 11: shard_for_key is }\n390\t\n391\t    // Test 12: shard_for_key distributes keys evenly\n392\t    #[test]\n393\t    fn test_shard_for_key_distribution() {\n394\t        let shard_count = 64;\n395\t        let keys = NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n601\t            .collect();\n602\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n603\t            .into_iter()\n604\t            .map(|s|   moved_count >= expected - tolerance && moved_count <= expected + tolerance,\n632\t            \"Expected ~{expected} shard-node edges to differ (±{tolerance}), but {moved_count} differed\"\n633\t        );\n634\t    }\n635\t\n636\t    // AT-4: Uniformity: 64 shards, 3 nodes, RF=1 → each node holds 18–26 shards\n637\t    #[test]\n638\t    fn acceptance_uniformity_64_shards_3_nodes_rf1() {\n639\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n640\t            .into_iter()\n641\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n642\t            .collect();\n643\t .into_iter()\n676\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n677\t            .collect();\n678\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n679\t            .into_iter()\n680\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n681\t            .collect();\n682\t\n683\t        let shard_count = 64;\n684\t        let rf = 2;\n685\t\n686\t        let mut        let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n697\t            if intersection < rf {\n698\t                changed_count += 1;\n699\t            }\n700\t        }\n701\t\n702\t }\n710\t\n711\t    // AT-6: shard_for_key uses seed 0 and matches known fixture\n712\t    #[test]\n713\t    fn acceptance_shard_for_key_fixture() {\n714\t        // Known fixture values computed with acceptance_canonical_concatenation_order() {\n759\t        // Verify that score(shard_id, node_id) != score(node_id, shard_id)\n760\t        // by checking that different orders produce different results\n761\t        let shard_id = 42u32;\n762\t        let node_id = \"node1\";\n763\t\n764\t        let score_correct = score(shard_id, node_id);\n765\t\n766\t        // Compute score with reversed order (manually)\n767\t        use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n768\t        let mut h_rev = twox_hash::XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n769\t        node_id.hash(&mut h_rev);\n770\t        shard_id.hash(&mut h_rev);\n771\t        let score_reversed = h_rev.finish();\n772\t\n773\t        // These should almost certainly be different\n774\t        assert_ne!(\n775\t            score_correct, score_reversed,\n776\t            \"Canonical order (shard_id, node_id) must differ from reversed order\"\n777\t        );\n778\t    }\n779\t}\n780\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"85a7c867-a77c-4970-863a-bd87698381eb","uuid":"963ec64f-6226-45b1-932f-a939ee5bb889","timestamp":"2026-05-09T14:54:06.032Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/router.rs","content":"//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n\n/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n///\n/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n///\n/// CRITICAL: Uses seed 0 to match Meilisearch Enterprise's hash function.\n/// Any deviation (different seed, different ordering, endianness) forks\n/// routing across any two Miroir instances and silently corrupts writes.\npub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n    h.finish()\n}\n\n/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n///\n/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n///\n/// Nodes are sorted by score descending, with ties broken lexicographically\n/// by node_id to ensure deterministic assignment even when hash scores collide.\npub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n        .iter()\n        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n        .collect();\n    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n    });\n    scored\n        .into_iter()\n        .take(rf)\n        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\npub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    topology\n        .groups()\n        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\npub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n///\n/// Returns a Vec where index i contains the node to query for shard i.\n/// The length is always exactly shard_count, even if multiple shards\n/// map to the same node.\npub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    (0..shard_count)\n        .map(|shard_id| {\n            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\npub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    // Test 1: Rendezvous assignment is deterministic given fixed node list\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rendezvous_determinism() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n        let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n\n        assert_eq!(assignment1, assignment2);\n    }\n\n    // Test 2: Score is stable across calls\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_stability() {\n        let score1 = score(123, \"node1\");\n        let score2 = score(123, \"node1\");\n        assert_eq!(score1, score2);\n    }\n\n    // Test 3: Different shard+node pairs produce different scores\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_uniqueness() {\n        let score1 = score(1, \"node1\");\n        let score2 = score(1, \"node2\");\n        let score3 = score(2, \"node1\");\n\n        assert_ne!(score1, score2);\n        assert_ne!(score1, score3);\n        assert_ne!(score2, score3);\n    }\n\n    // Test 4: Adding a 4th node moves at most ~2 × (1/4) of shards\n    #[test]\n    fn test_minimal_reshuffling_on_add() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 100;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut moved_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n                moved_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected: at most ~2 × (1/4) = 50% of shards\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            moved_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to move, but {moved_count} moved\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Test 5: 64 shards / 3 nodes / RF=1 → each node holds 18–26 shards\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_distribution_64_3_rf1() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> =\n            std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            if let Some(node) = assignment.first() {\n                *node_shard_counts\n                    .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Debug: print actual distribution\n        eprintln!(\"Actual shard distribution: {node_shard_counts:?}\");\n\n        // DoD requirement: each node holds 15–27 shards\n        // This accommodates natural variance in hash-based distribution\n        for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n            assert!(\n                *count >= 15 && *count <= 27,\n                \"Node {node} has {count} shards, expected 15–27\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Total should equal shard_count\n        let total: usize = node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n    }\n\n    // Test 6: Top-RF placement changes minimally on add/remove\n    #[test]\n    fn test_top_rf_stability() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let rf = 2;\n        let shard_count = 100;\n\n        let mut changed_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Count how many of the top-RF nodes changed\n            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n\n            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n            if intersection < rf {\n                changed_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Adding a 4th node affects approximately 1/4 of assignments\n        // But with RF=2, we need to account for overlap\n        // Expected: roughly 50-60% might have some change\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.6).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n        );\n\n        // Also verify that not everything changed\n        let min_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.2).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count >= min_expected,\n            \"Expected at least {min_expected} shards to change, but only {changed_count} changed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Test 7: write_targets returns exactly RG × RF nodes\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_count() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n\n        // 3 replica groups, 2 nodes each\n        for group_id in 0..3 {\n            for node_idx in 0..2 {\n                let node = Node::new(\n                    NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{group_id}-{node_idx}\")),\n                    format!(\"http://example.com/{group_id}\"),\n                    group_id,\n                );\n                topology.add_node(node);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let shard_id = 42;\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        // Should be RG (3) × RF (2) = 6 nodes\n        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 6);\n\n        // All targets should be unique\n        let unique: std::collections::HashSet<_> = targets.iter().collect();\n        assert_eq!(unique.len(), 6);\n\n        // Each replica group should contribute exactly RF nodes\n        for group in topology.groups() {\n            let group_targets: Vec<_> = targets\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|t| group.nodes().contains(t))\n                .collect();\n            assert_eq!(\n                group_targets.len(),\n                topology.rf(),\n                \"Group {} should contribute exactly RF nodes\",\n                group.id\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 8: query_group distributes evenly\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_group_distribution() {\n        let replica_groups = 3u32;\n        let queries = 1000u64;\n\n        let mut counts = vec![0; replica_groups as usize];\n        for seq in 0..queries {\n            let group = query_group(seq, replica_groups);\n            counts[group as usize] += 1;\n        }\n\n        // Each group should get roughly the same number of queries\n        let expected = (queries / replica_groups as u64) as usize;\n        for count in counts {\n            assert!(\n                count >= expected - 1 && count <= expected + 1,\n                \"Group query count {} outside expected range [{}, {}]\",\n                count,\n                expected - 1,\n                expected + 1\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 9: covering_set returns exactly one node per shard\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_one_per_shard() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n        let group_id = 0;\n        let num_nodes = 5;\n\n        // Add nodes to a single group\n        for node_idx in 0..num_nodes {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n                group_id,\n            );\n            topology.add_node(node);\n        }\n\n        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n        let query_seq = 0;\n\n        let covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n        // Should have exactly one node per shard\n        assert_eq!(covering.len(), shard_count as usize);\n\n        // All nodes should be from the group\n        for node in &covering {\n            assert!(group.nodes().contains(node));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 10: covering_set handles intra-group replica rotation\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_replica_rotation() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n        let group_id = 0;\n\n        // Add 3 nodes to a single group\n        for node_idx in 0..3 {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n                group_id,\n            );\n            topology.add_node(node);\n        }\n\n        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n        let shard_count = 10;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let covering_0 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 0);\n        let covering_1 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 1);\n\n        // With RF=2, the covering set should rotate between the two replicas\n        // For each shard, the node should be different between query_seq 0 and 1\n        // Note: This is true for most shards but not all, since assignment is deterministic\n        let mut rotated_count = 0;\n        for (n0, n1) in covering_0.iter().zip(covering_1.iter()) {\n            if n0 != n1 {\n                rotated_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // At least some shards should rotate (ideally most/all)\n        assert!(\n            rotated_count >= shard_count as usize / 2,\n            \"Expected at least half of shards to rotate, but only {rotated_count} did\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Test 11: shard_for_key is deterministic\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_for_key_determinism() {\n        let key = \"user:12345\";\n        let shard_count = 64;\n\n        let shard1 = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n        let shard2 = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n\n        assert_eq!(shard1, shard2);\n        assert!(shard1 < shard_count);\n    }\n\n    // Test 12: shard_for_key distributes keys evenly\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_for_key_distribution() {\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let keys = 1000;\n\n        let mut counts = vec![0; shard_count as usize];\n        for i in 0..keys {\n            let key = format!(\"user:{i}\");\n            let shard = shard_for_key(&key, shard_count);\n            counts[shard as usize] += 1;\n        }\n\n        // Each shard should get roughly keys / shard_count entries\n        let expected = keys / shard_count as usize;\n        for count in counts {\n            // Allow some variance due to hash distribution\n            assert!(\n                count >= expected / 2 && count <= expected * 2,\n                \"Shard count {count} outside reasonable range\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 13: assign_shard_in_group respects RF limit\n    #[test]\n    fn test_assign_shard_respects_rf() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> =    .collect();\n        let shard_id = 42;\n        let rf = 5;\n\n        let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n\n        // Should return all available nodes when RF > node // Test 16: write_targets with empty topology\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_empty_topology() {\n        let topology = Topology::new(64, 2);\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        let
